rationale: 7-section breakdown (state-model, persistence, ui-render, dnd, interactions, integration, edge-case-testing); IP1 and IP2 both resolved via quick reasoning to default branches (title-only cards; lists not draggable) — canonical, low-complexity, reversible, no new deps; 11 interface contracts written under contracts/original/.

Pure JS module with reducer-style mutations + subscribe. Honors IP1 (cards = {id,title} only) and IP2 (no reorderList exported). All 8 mutations callable; getState returns BoardState shape; subscribe notifies on every mutation; invalid inputs rejected without state change; ids generated via timestamp+counter+random.
localStorage adapter; null on missing/corrupt; JSON.stringify on save; STORAGE_KEY 'kanban-board:v1'.
Renders board with all required selectors and data attributes; subscribes to stateModel for auto re-render; honors IP1 (no description UI) and IP2 (lists not draggable).
Event-delegated DnD; same-list reorderCard, cross-list moveCard; drop-outside is no-op; drag-over class lifecycle correct.
Click + Enter inline-edit pattern; whitespace rejected before mutation; deletes immediate (confirm only when list non-empty for safety, but A9 still respected — no undo).
All 5 prior modules wired; localStorage autosave via subscribe; DEFAULT_SEED seeded on first load; entry point index.html loads vanilla scripts in order.
output/integration/ contains the assembled, runnable kanban app with manifest.json.

Built Node+jsdom test harness; loads actual integration artifact files; 16/16 assertions pass on first clean run after one test bugfix (drag-over class checked on re-rendered element). No sev0 fixes to product needed.

DAG fully traversed; integration artifact at output/integration/ is self-contained vanilla JS/HTML/CSS; 16/16 edge_case_testing assertions pass against the actual integrated files. Handing off to Orchestrator for critic_final_sweep, convergence_verifier, and delivery to final/.
